sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते।
तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि॥
transliteration:
indriyāṇāṃ hi caratāṃ yan mano’nuvidhīyate
tadasya harati prajñāṃ vāyurnāvam ivāmbhasi
literal_meaning: Of the senses, indeed, while they roam, whatever the mind follows—that alone carries away his discernment, as wind (carries away) a boat on water.
contextual_meaning: Attention is the rudder of consciousness; when even one sense-object hooks the mind, the whole cognitive boat is dragged off-course, dissolving mature judgment into impulsive action.
psychological_state: Pre-decisional paralysis: intellect overwhelmed by a single compelling stimulus, mirroring compulsive checking, binge-scrolling, or addictive loops.
trigger_scenario: You open your phone to answer one text, see a single tantalizing headline, and thirty minutes later realize you've forgotten why you picked it up—your original intention literally blown off-course.
anchor_text: Keep your mind anchored or the senses will steal your clarity.
